directory from having

Write bytes to file. Fails with FILE_EXISTS if the file already exists (exclusive create).

Contract obligations:
- **Parent-directory creation:** the adapter MUST ensure parent directories exist before the
 exclusive write. Equivalent to

. If the
 parent is removed between the implicit mkdir and the open (e.g. concurrent

prunes
 the fanout), the adapter retries once: re-create the parent, re-attempt the open. On a second
 ENOENT the error propagates as FILE_NOT_FOUND.
- **Symlink-safe ancestor check:** the adapter MUST reject writes where any ancestor directory
 of

is a symbolic link whose resolved target is outside the containment root. This
 closes the attack where an attacker replaces

with a symlink pointing elsewhere.
 Implementation: lstat-walk the ancestor chain, or use

falsy): every path is

and embedded repositories are skipped.

once, read the existing index under
the lock, and commit a single replacement — no partial writes.

Abort the cherry-pick: hard-reset the working tree, index, and branch to the
pre-sequence HEAD (sequencer

, or the current HEAD for a lone pick), and
clear all state. The branch update records git's faithful
reflog. Refuses when nothing is in progress.

Finalise the in-progress conflicted pick as a single-parent commit from the
resolved index, then resume any remaining sequencer picks. Refuses when nothing
is in progress or the index is unmerged. A no-change resolution re-stops empty.

Drop the in-progress pick: hard-reset to HEAD (discarding its half-applied
state), then resume the remaining sequencer picks (if any).

Create a new commit from the current index. Resolves author/committer from
options or

, writes a tree from the index, builds the
commit object, and updates HEAD's target ref (or detached HEAD itself).

when the new tree matches the parent's tree and

when the message is blank

for the second parent, falls back to

when the
message is empty, runs hooks, and clears merge state on success
(ADR-174).

- Up-to-date: target is ancestor of HEAD → no-op.
- Fast-forward: HEAD is ancestor of target → branch advances.
- True merge for diverged histories:.4a wired the three-way
 tree merge (

) so a CLEAN merge commits

 MERGE_MSG / ORIG_HEAD) and returns

.

— the resulting commit has two parents.
Move/rename tracked paths in the index and the working tree, faithful to

. Each (source → target) pair is validated up front; on any refusal
nothing is moved (unless

). Blob ids and modes are copied from the
source's index entry — the working file is renamed as-is, never re-hashed.

Abort the rebase: hard-reset the working tree + index to the pre-rebase tip,
reattach

reflog
(the branch never moved during the replay, so it gets no entry), clear state.

Resume the rebase after the conflicted commit was resolved: commit the resolved
index as the stopped commit (preserved author from
reflog), then replay the remaining todo.
Drop the conflicted commit (hard-reset to the last good pick) and replay the rest.
Reset HEAD (and optionally index/working tree) to

: HEAD + rebuild the index from the target commit's tree
 (stat-cache donor strategy preserves cache for unchanged paths —
). Working tree is NOT touched.
-

: HEAD + index + working tree — materialise the target tree onto
 the working tree with

, then commit the post-write
 index entries. Bare repos are rejected upfront.

Ordering: working tree → index → HEAD. The index lock wraps both the
working-tree write AND the index commit, matching tightened
pattern. A crash between the index commit and HEAD update leaves the index
ahead of HEAD — same recoverable hazard as canonical git.

Abort the revert: hard-reset the working tree, index, and branch to the
pre-sequence HEAD (sequencer

, or the current HEAD for a lone revert),
and clear all state. The branch update records git's faithful
reflog. Refuses when nothing is in progress.

Finalise the in-progress conflicted revert (if any) as a single-parent commit,
then resume the remaining sequencer reverts. Refuses when nothing is in progress
or the index is unmerged; a resolution that yields no change re-stops empty.

Drop the in-progress revert: hard-reset to HEAD (discarding its half-applied
state), then resume the remaining sequencer reverts (if any).
Remove the given paths from the index (and from the working tree unless
). Refuses when a path isn't tracked, matches
(default newest) onto the working tree via a 3-way merge.
then drop it; on conflict the stash is retained.

Summarize the state of the working tree relative to the index, and the
branch HEAD points at. Working-tree-vs-HEAD diff (Git's "staged" column)
is approximated via index-vs-working-tree comparisons until
adds the stat-cache fast path.

Progress reporting: emits

start before the
fan-out, updates at every 100 lstat completions, and end in a finally
block so the consumer always pairs start with end.

is undefined
— design choice that prevents revealing repository size to non-trusted
progress sinks.

Sparse-checkout filter. When supplied, an excluded path is rebuilt as a
zero-stat skip-worktree entry and an in-pattern path has any stale
skip-worktree bit cleared.

⇒ sparse inactive — the rebuilt
index is byte-identical to the non-sparse behaviour.
Hex SHA of the pack body, also the trailer (last 20 bytes).

call so a long
batch over untrusted ids cannot exhaust the heap. Defaults to no cap.
Per-object byte cap. Rejected pre-inflate; same semantics as

. When unset, no cap applies (parity
with
Object ids the remote already has (server's advertised ref tips).
Hard cap on objects emitted. Defaults to MAX_PUSH_OBJECTS.

Object ids the caller wants on the remote. Each MUST resolve to a
commit or an annotated tag (tags are unwrapped to commits before the
commit walk).
Shallow clone depth. When set, sends
Partial-clone object filter — a canonical filter spec. When set, a
line is emitted; the caller must have negotiated the
Objects the caller already has (negotiation). Empty for clone, populated for fetch.
Progress op label — clone uses 'clone:write-objects', fetch uses 'fetch:write-objects'.

sentinel beside the pack
so the objects it references but omits are treated as promised.
Base remote URL (the same URL passed to clone).
Advertised refs the caller wants. MUST be non-empty (server-side requirement).
Hex-encoded SHA of the pack trailer; also the on-disk filename stem.
Commits the server advertised as new shallow boundaries (empty when depth is unset).
Commits the server advertised as no-longer-shallow (empty when depth is unset).
Optional arguments and stdin a caller threads into a hook invocation.

block of a git-config-format INI file: the
section name, an optional quoted subsection, and its key/value entries.
Exported so

parsing — byte-identical grammar — reuses one
tokenizer (ADR-086).
POSIX-relative directory whose file carried the rule.
1-based line number of the matching rule inside its file.

When true, every target-tree path is written to the working tree
unconditionally — even paths the index→target diff classified as

, where the working
tree may diverge from the index (uncommitted local modifications); the
standard index→target diff cannot see that drift, so noops would skip
paths the caller wants overwritten. Default

keeps the Phase
13.1 checkout behaviour: clean files are never spuriously rewritten.
Branch-switch sparse-checkout filter. Honoured ONLY when

is

Contract obligations:
- **Parent-directory creation:** the adapter MUST ensure parent directories exist before the
 exclusive write. Equivalent to

. If the
 parent is removed between the implicit mkdir and the open (e.g. concurrent

prunes
 the fanout), the adapter retries once: re-create the parent, re-attempt the open. On a second
 ENOENT the error propagates as FILE_NOT_FOUND.
- **Symlink-safe ancestor check:** the adapter MUST reject writes where any ancestor directory
 of

is a symbolic link whose resolved target is outside the containment root. This
 closes the attack where an attacker replaces

with a symlink pointing elsewhere.

Options shared by every resolver port. The

Resolves a tree object by its oid. Caching adapters use a content-addressed
LRU (oids are immutable, no invalidation needed). Throws on type
mismatch — the oid must address a tree.
Streams working-tree entries in canonical git path order. Honors

) down to this shape; the enumerator port itself stays

Command side of the write-event triple (ADR-157). Write-boundary primitives
depend on this interface only — they cannot observe events, only emit them.

Implementations MUST be called AFTER a successful write but BEFORE
releasing any acquired lock. See

for the
lock-ordering protocol; in-process readers rely on the
emit-before-release ordering to avoid TOCTOU windows.

Subscribe side of the write-event triple (ADR-157). Cache adapters depend
on this interface only — they observe events but cannot emit them.

Subscribers receive identical event sequences regardless of subscription
order. Disposing a subscription removes the listener; the next event is
not delivered to that listener.
Authentication strategy for transport — narrowed shape consumed by withAuth.

The lifecycle hooks tsgit invokes. Extend the union to add a hook.

before reaching

Methods that the user did not supply are absent on the wrapper (preserves

and dropped — a faulty logger must not crash the operation.

readme.text:
# tsgit

[![CI](https://github.com/scolladon/tsgit/actions/workflows/ci.yml/badge.svg)](https://github.com/scolladon/tsgit/actions/workflows/ci.yml)
[![npm](https://img.shields.io/npm/v/@scolladon/tsgit)](https://www.npmjs.com/package/@scolladon/tsgit)
[![License: MIT](https://img.shields.io/badge/License-MIT-blue.svg)](LICENSE)

**Lightning-fast git, pure TypeScript, everywhere.**

Pure TypeScript git — Node.js, browser, Deno, Bun, and Cloudflare Workers. Zero native deps. Zero WASM. Stable v1, semver-tracked — see [BACKLOG](

)

## Capabilities

- Zero runtime dependencies — no transitive surface
- Pure TypeScript — no native code, no WASM, no

binary required
- Cross-runtime — Node 22+ · Deno · Bun · Cloudflare Workers · Browser (OPFS) · in-memory
- Tree-shakeable —

; each primitive is an independent entry
- CJS + ESM dual-publish, verified by
- 30 Tier-1 commands · 20+ AsyncIterable primitives · operator toolkit (

, …)
- Snapshot+join surface — query tree / index / workdir / stash through one lazy, atomic-iteration pipeline ([primer](

) — Node, browser, memory, migration from isomorphic-git
- 🛠️ [Use it](
